item.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures slip amid continued Iran fallout
item.description: Futures on major U.S. stock indices, the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq, slipped. This decline is attributed to the continued fallout from the Iran conflict.

item.title: Federal Reserve Board announces termination of enforcement actions with Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Ltd., Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Ltd., New York Branch, Standard Chartered PLC, and Standard Chartered Bank
item.description: The Federal Reserve Board announced the termination of enforcement actions with Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Ltd. and Standard Chartered PLC.

item.title: A Sprawling Middle East War Explodes
item.description: A joint U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran, followed by Iranian retaliation, has engulfed much of the Middle East in conflict.

item.title: Operation Epic Folly? Trump’s and Netanyahu’s War on Iran
item.description: Crisis Group experts discuss the U.S.-Israel war with Iran, its fallout in the Gulf and wider region, and what might come next.

item.title: Middle East crisis could push UK inflation back up to 3%, says OBR
item.description: The UK's Office for Budget Responsibility (OBR) believes inflation could end 2026 at 3% due to the U.S.-Israel war in Iran.

item.title: Oil Supply Risks Mount as Iran Lays Mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran has begun laying naval mines in the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, a move that could extend the effective blockade of the world’s most critical energy chokepoint.

item.title: IEA Mulls Emergency Action To Unleash Oil Reserves
item.description: Global energy leaders are gathering to decide whether to release emergency oil reserves as production and export disruptions tied to the Middle East conflict continue to strain global markets.
